title: Anatomy of a Self-Referential Structure
description: A visual breakdown of a linked list node, showing how a structure contains a pointer to another instance of its own type, enabling the creation of dynamic data structures.
relationships: The Node Structure contains a Data Field and a Pointer Field. The Pointer Field holds the memory address of the next Node Structure, creating a chain. The final Pointer Field points to NULL to signify the end of the list.
